Zone: Integration Adapter Layer (`integration-adapter-layer`)
Files: 16, Cohesion: 0.38, Coupling: 0.63

<findings>

[observation] [warning] High coupling (0.63) — 18 imports target "prd-domain-operations"
[observation] [warning] Low cohesion (0.38) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
[observation] [warning] Cohesion 0.38 and coupling 0.63 are both at or beyond warning thresholds; the zone bundles CLI sync dispatch, adapter registry, and multiple integration schema implementations into one community, weakening its single-responsibility profile.
[observation] [warning] src/cli/commands/sync.ts sits inside a store-dominated zone rather than the CLI zone, creating an implicit cross-tier dependency that bypasses the gateway pattern.
[suggestion] [info] Zone "integration-adapter-layer" has files across 7 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
[suggestion] [warning] RISK AREA: cohesion 0.38 + coupling 0.63 simultaneously exceed warning thresholds and the zone spans two non-adjacent tiers — this combination means any change to either sync dispatch or adapter registry propagates across both CLI and domain tiers unpredictably; freeze new additions to this zone until it is split
[suggestion] [info] Rename zone from 'integration-adapter-layer' to 'integration-infrastructure' or split into 'sync-cli-command' + 'adapter-registry' to eliminate the tier-composition mismatch implied by the 'layer' suffix
[suggestion] [warning] Zone "Integration Adapter Layer" (integration-adapter-layer) has critical risk (score: 0.63, cohesion: 0.38, coupling: 0.63) — requires refactoring before new feature development
[relationship] [warning] integration-adapter-layer and the store zone (other zones) together fragment adapter infrastructure across two Louvain communities — files sharing the same architectural role (adapter registry, schemas, Notion adapter) are split between zones, meaning no single zone boundary maps cleanly to the integration subsystem and zone health metrics undercount the true coupling surface.
[anti-pattern] [warning] Zone spans two non-adjacent architectural tiers (CLI dispatch + domain persistence) simultaneously — no single cohesion metric can accurately describe a zone whose files have fundamentally different dependency profiles; the zone must be split before health metrics become meaningful
[anti-pattern] [critical] sync.ts imports directly from ../../store/index.js, bypassing any interface boundary; the CLI command knows the internal shape of the store rather than depending on an abstract SyncPort — this is a missing ports-and-adapters boundary, not just a misplaced file

</findings>

<insights>

- Low cohesion (0.38) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- High coupling (0.63) — 18 imports target "prd-domain-operations"
- The sync CLI command (sync.ts) is co-located with store internals, blurring the CLI/domain boundary — consider pinning it to the CLI zone or extracting it to a dedicated sync-command file group
- Integration schemas for Jira and Notion share structural patterns (field mappings, auth config) that could be unified under a single abstract schema base to reduce per-adapter boilerplate
- The adapter registry acts as a plugin system — document the contract explicitly so future adapters (Linear, GitHub Issues) can be added without touching core store logic
- Cohesion 0.38 and coupling 0.63 are both at or beyond warning thresholds; the zone bundles CLI sync dispatch, adapter registry, and multiple integration schema implementations into one community, weakening its single-responsibility profile.
- src/cli/commands/sync.ts sits inside a store-dominated zone rather than the CLI zone, creating an implicit cross-tier dependency that bypasses the gateway pattern.
- Zone "integration-adapter-layer" has files across 7 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- The store zone (cohesion 0.38, listed in other zones) and integration-adapter-layer both carry store-layer files; the two zones together fragment the adapter subsystem across separate communities, creating an implicit dependency split that is invisible to gateway enforcement.
- integration-adapter-layer and the store zone (other zones) together fragment adapter infrastructure across two Louvain communities — files sharing the same architectural role (adapter registry, schemas, Notion adapter) are split between zones, meaning no single zone boundary maps cleanly to the integration subsystem and zone health metrics undercount the true coupling surface.
- sync.ts directly imports from ../../store/index.js, making the CLI command tightly coupled to store internals rather than going through a domain interface — this is not just a zone placement issue but a missing abstraction: the CLI should interact with sync capabilities through a SyncPort interface, not raw store imports
- The zone simultaneously spans the CLI dispatch tier (sync.ts) and domain persistence tier (adapter-registry, notion-adapter, schemas) — it is the only zone in the package that owns files from two non-adjacent architectural tiers, making it impossible to enforce tier-boundary rules without splitting it first
- sync.ts imports directly from ../../store/index.js, bypassing any interface boundary; the CLI command knows the internal shape of the store rather than depending on an abstract SyncPort — this is a missing ports-and-adapters boundary, not just a misplaced file
- Zone spans two non-adjacent architectural tiers (CLI dispatch + domain persistence) simultaneously — no single cohesion metric can accurately describe a zone whose files have fundamentally different dependency profiles; the zone must be split before health metrics become meaningful
- Zone name uses the 'layer' suffix implying pure infrastructure, but contains sync.ts which is a CLI dispatch file — the name actively misleads about the zone's tier composition and should be 'integration-adapter-and-sync-command' or split into two correctly-named zones
- The word 'layer' in the zone ID implies a single architectural tier, but this is the only zone in the package whose name claims to be a layer while actually spanning two non-adjacent tiers — all other multi-tier zones have neutral names
- Rename zone from 'integration-adapter-layer' to 'integration-infrastructure' or split into 'sync-cli-command' + 'adapter-registry' to eliminate the tier-composition mismatch implied by the 'layer' suffix
- RISK AREA: cohesion 0.38 + coupling 0.63 simultaneously exceed warning thresholds and the zone spans two non-adjacent tiers — this combination means any change to either sync dispatch or adapter registry propagates across both CLI and domain tiers unpredictably; freeze new additions to this zone until it is split
- [call graph] 332 internal calls, 47 outgoing, 52 incoming (cohesion: 0.88, coupling: 0.12)

</insights>
